channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pentagon’s Dispute with Anthropic Highlights Challenges for Startups in Defense Work
item.description: In March 2026, negotiations between the Pentagon and AI startup Anthropic over the use of its Claude technology collapsed, leading to the Trump administration labeling the company a supply-chain risk....

item.title: 30 Years of South Africa’s Constitution: Reflecting on Progress and Renewing Commitment
item.description: This year marks the 30th anniversary of South Africa's Constitution, signed into law in Sharpeville on Human Rights Day, 1996. The Constitution remains the cornerstone of the nation’s democracy, embod...

item.title: Kenya Records 42 Flood Deaths; Government Deploys Aid to Affected Communities
item.description: Heavy rains on Friday evening caused severe flooding across several regions in Kenya, leaving a death toll of 42, according to the Ministry of Special Programmes. Nairobi recorded the highest fataliti...

item.title: Malacañang warns against profiteering and hoarding as Middle East conflict drives up oil and commodity prices
item.description: Malacañang, through Communications Secretary Claire Castro, has reiterated President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s call for unity among Filipinos amid the ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The ...

item.title: UAE Intercepts and Destroys Iranian Drones Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: United Arab Emirates air defences successfully intercepted and destroyed several Iranian drones targeting the country. Dramatic footage released by the UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs shows the drones...

item.title: NetNaija CEO Arraigned Over Copyright Infringement, Bail Hearing Set for March 9
item.description: Omo, Emmanuel Analike, the CEO of NetNaija wey be one of Nigeria's biggest sites for free movies and music downloads, don waka go Federal High Court Abuja. Him dey face multiple charges of copyright i...

item.title: How US–Israeli strikes on Iranian oil depots caused toxic rainfall and long‑term pollution risks
item.description: The article explains reports of so‑called ‘acid rain’ in Iran that appeared shortly after US–Israeli airstrikes on oil storage and refinery facilities, and it unpacks what is really happening in the a...

item.title: ASX reacts sharply as oil prices surge amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Australian markets opened Monday amid escalating concerns over the conflict in Iran, triggering significant investor anxiety. Almost immediately, West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil prices jumped ...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Confirms Payment Systems Are Secure Amid Economic Growth
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) has reassured the public that all national payment systems are secure, fully operational, and protected against fraud and cyber threats. During the BoT Iftar, Governor Emman...

item.title: Governor R.N. Ravi’s Tenure Ends Amid Political Developments in Tamil Nadu
item.description: The controversial tenure of Governor R.N. Ravi in Tamil Nadu has come to an unexpected end as the Union Home Ministry carried out a gubernatorial reshuffle ahead of the upcoming state elections. Durin...

item.title: Nairobi Floods Kill 42 and Displace Over 50,000 After Weekend Downpours
item.description: Nairobi experienced unusually heavy rainfall over the past weekend, which resulted in devastating floods affecting multiple areas of the city and other parts of Kenya. According to the Kenya Meteorolo...

item.title: Ring CEO Addresses Privacy Concerns Over New AI Camera Features
item.description: Ring founder and CEO Jamie Siminoff faced a wave of privacy concerns after the company’s first Super Bowl ad introduced Search Party, an AI-powered feature that uses Ring camera footage to locate lost...

item.title: Strong winds cause widespread power outages in northern B.C.
item.description: Strong winds sweeping across northern British Columbia have left thousands without electricity, affecting over 14,000 customers in regions including the central interior, Okanagan, Kootenays, and nort...

item.title: Top Headlines from Nigerian Newspapers: Police Leadership Change, 2027 Election Warnings, Women's Representation Push, and More - 9 March 2026
item.description: Good morning, Nigeria! This Naija News roundup bring you the major headlines for Monday, 9th March 2026. First, the new Inspector-General of Police, Olatunji Rilwan Disu, don remove the outgoing Force...

item.title: Voter registration for BARMM parliamentary polls reaches nearly 49,000
item.description: As voter registration continues for the upcoming Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections scheduled in November, the Commission on Elections (Comelec) reported t...

item.title: UK job market shows limited recovery amid economic uncertainty
item.description: Recent data indicates that the UK job market remains weak, with minimal signs of recovery. Companies continue to exercise caution in hiring due to economic uncertainty and cost pressures. The BDO mont...

item.title: Kenyan businesses face challenges due to Persian Gulf conflict
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Persian Gulf is hitting Kenyan businesses hard, particularly those involved in trade with the United Arab Emirates. Tea exporters and livestock traders are experiencing del...

item.title: Daily Iftar and Sehri Timings for Major Cities of Pakistan
item.description: This news article provides the Iftar and Sehri timings for Ramazan 19 and 20 for all major federal and provincial capitals in Pakistan. In Islamabad, according to Fiqh-i-Hanafia, today's Iftar is at 6...

item.title: District Administration Imposes Section 144 in Dera Ismail Khan to Maintain Law and Order
item.description: PESHAWAR: The district administration of Dera Ismail Khan in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has imposed Section 144 due to the current law and order situation, restricting movement on key roads and sensitive area...

item.title: IFP calls for expulsion of illegal foreign nationals in eThekwini amid immigration corruption revelations
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) in eThekwini has called for stricter enforcement of South Africa's immigration laws and the removal of illegal foreign nationals from Durban. This position was expresse...
